outcome_contract.why: Security audits are required before any site goes live. There is currently no security-audit domain in the S9B launch QA gate, no structured runtime/site-level security checklist in the startup loop, and the CI-level checks that exist are absent from app-specific deploy workflows.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Every site progressing through the startup loop to launch is checked against a structured security baseline (OWASP top 10 runtime/headers, dependency CVEs, secrets, auth hardening, cookie security) before it goes live, with a hard gate preventing S9B→SIGNALS advance when critical security failures are present.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: N/A but adds second CLI invocation

engineering_coverage.applicable: Adds 8 OWASP runtime checks as a domain; hard blocks on critical failures
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Adds same checks but decoupled from report synthesis
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Domain model: checks + cross-domain synthesis in one pass

engineering_coverage.applicable: Security checks emit into QA report alongside all other domains
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Security results in a separate report; must be manually cross-referenced
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Unified report; loop-state updated with security verdict

engineering_coverage.applicable: Follows untested domain module convention; live QA run is integration test
Domain output schema unchanged; loop-spec version bump; cmd-advance.md two-entry update
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Requires new skill contract, new S9B secondary_skills entry, separate go/no-go logic
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Minimal contract surface change; additive only

engineering_coverage.applicable: Additive; gate only fires on new/re-run QA; existing loop-state checkpoints unaffected
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: More complex — two skills to roll back if issues arise
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Clean rollback: revert domain-security.md, revert cmd-advance.md entries

stage_payload.decision_summary: The loop currently sends sites live without any structured security check. The S9B launch QA gate (`/lp-launch-qa`) has 6 domains (conversion, SEO, performance, legal, brand-copy, measurement) but no security domain. CI has dependency audit and secret scanning for core packages but brikette's standalone pipeline excludes both. The decision is **how to add security as a first-class gate** — whether as an extension of `lp-launch-qa`, as a standalone new skill, or purely in CI.
goals: Security baseline verified before any site goes live (OWASP runtime controls, dependency CVEs, secrets, auth hardening, CSP/headers).
Hard gate at S9B→SIGNALS: critical security failures block advance.
CI dependency audit and secret scanning on every app-specific deploy pipeline.
non_goals: Penetration testing or manual red-team exercises.
assumptions: Runtime HTTP header checks can be performed by the QA subagent using curl against the deployed site URL (available from `latest.user.md`).
Pre-existing high/critical CVEs will be remediated as part of this plan before TASK-05 CI changes can pass.

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: Gate must actually block S9B→SIGNALS, not just document a check
New components must follow existing patterns (domain module schema, cmd-advance module pattern)
Must apply to all businesses, not just brikette
Dependency audit and secret scanning must run on every app deploy
Launch QA report must be unified (one run, all domains including security)
Avoid creating infrastructure or abstractions beyond what this plan needs
Post-launch security debt should surface as IDEAS items

options_considered.Description: Add `domain-security.md` as 7th domain; add `cmd-advance/s9b-gates.md` for enforcement; extend CI for brikette and reusable pipeline; loop-spec GATE-LAUNCH-SEC annotation.
options_considered.Upside: Consistent with existing architecture; unified QA report; proven pattern; cross-domain synthesis includes security.
options_considered.Downside: Enforcement requires four file changes: new gate module + cmd-advance.md two entries + SKILL.md modules table + SKILL.md version references.
options_considered.Key risks: Runtime HTTP checks require curl/tooling available in QA subagent context.

options_considered.Description: Create a new dedicated skill separate from lp-launch-qa; S9B invokes both skills.
options_considered.Downside: Fragments the unified domain model; lp-launch-qa's cross-domain synthesis cannot see security signals; duplicates orchestration infrastructure; complicates loop-spec S9B definition.
options_considered.Key risks: Double-maintenance burden; go/no-go aggregation splits across two skills.

options_considered.Description: Rely entirely on CI (pnpm audit, TruffleHog, OWASP header check in pipeline) with no lp-launch-qa changes.
options_considered.Downside: CI runs before deployment; runtime site-level checks (headers, CSP, cookie flags) require a running site — structurally invalid for a pre-deploy CI check. No operator-facing security report. No loop gate.
options_considered.Key risks: Sites launch after CI passes but before runtime security is verified.

options_considered.Description: Same as Option A but explicitly defer TASK-06 (self-evolving prescription) to a follow-on gap-fill dispatch.
options_considered.Upside: Tighter scope; no risk from incompletely understood prescription registry.
options_considered.Downside: Self-evolving stays blind to post-launch security debt.
options_considered.Key risks: Low — shadow mode means no live impact either way.

chosen_approach.Option A is the only architecturally con: it follows the proven `lp-launch-qa` domain module pattern across 6 existing reference implementations.
chosen_approach.Enforcement is complete: `cmd-advance/s9b-gates.md` (new gate module) + `cmd-advance.md` (Module Loading Order + Gate and Dispatch Map entries) + `startup-loop/SKILL.md` (internal modules table + spec_version references) constitute the full enforcement and contract-sync path identified in the fact-find critique loop.
chosen_approach.Loop-wide coverage: `reusable-app.yml` gets pnpm audit + TruffleHog (covers caryina including manual `workflow_dispatch` runs that bypass `ci.yml`); `brikette.yml` closes the brikette-specific gap (also excluded from `ci.yml`).
chosen_approach.Unified operator experience: one `/lp-launch-qa` run, one report, one go/no-go verdict.
chosen_approach.Self-evolving deferral is correct: shadow mode means no live impact either way, and a partial prescription registration without a confirmed schema adds noise without value.
chosen_approach.**Option B (standalone skill)** — reject: fragments the unified domain model; lp-launch-qa cross-domain synthesis cannot see security signals; doubles orchestration infrastructure with no benefit over extending an existing well-established pattern.
chosen_approach.**Option C (CI-only)** — rejected: structurally invalid for runtime site-level checks; no operator-facing security gate; CI cannot check HTTP headers, CSP, or cookie flags against a live deployed site.
chosen_approach.**TASK-06 in-scope** — deferred: self-evolving is shadow mode; prescription registry schema not fully confirmed; deferral removes uncertainty with no live impact cost.

rejected_approaches: **Option B (standalone skill)** — rejected: fragments the unified domain model; lp-launch-qa cross-domain synthesis cannot see security signals; doubles orchestration infrastructure with no benefit over extending an existing well-established pattern.

**Option C (CI-only)** — rejected: structurally invalid for runtime site-level checks; no operator-facing security gate; CI cannot check HTTP headers, CSP, or cookie flags against a live deployed site.
**TASK-06 in-scope** — deferred: self-evolving is shadow mode; prescription registry schema not fully confirmed; deferral removes uncertainty with no live impact cost.

areas.label: **S9B launch QA gate**
areas.current: 6 domain modules; no security domain; `/lp-launch-qa --domain all` dispatches 6 subagents
areas.future: 7 domain subagents dispatched in parallel (security added); security subagent checks HTTPS, headers, CSP, cookie flags, repo secrets, auth, CORS; result merged into unified QA report; GATE-LAUNCH-SEC failure = NO-GO
areas.steady: Domain module output schema, go/no-go aggregation, report artifact path, loop-state update
areas.seams: If site URL is unreachable: domain-security.md must set `status: fail` (not warn) — unverified security baseline is a failure.

areas.label: **S9B→SIGNALS advance gate**
areas.current: No S9B gate family in cmd-advance; SIGNALS advance proceeds without security check
areas.future: cmd-advance.md Module Loading Order includes s9b-gates.md for S9B→SIGNALS transition; GATE-LAUNCH-SEC checks for a passing QA report ≤30 days old with security domain `status: pass`; hard-blocks advance if security failed or no recent QA run
areas.steady: All other advance gate families unaffected
areas.seams: QA report age check (30-day window) must be defined in s9b-gates.md; check must be filesystem-only (no external HTTP call at advance time)

areas.label: **CI: brikette deploy pipeline**
areas.current: No pnpm audit, no TruffleHog in `brikette.yml`; `ci.yml` excludes brikette paths
areas.trigger: Push to `dev`, `staging`, or `main` with brikette-scoped changes
areas.future: `brikette.yml` runs `pnpm audit --audit-level=high` (hard fail) and TruffleHog (`--only-verified`) before the build/deploy jobs; pre-existing CVEs must be remediated before this step passes
areas.steady: Cloudflare Pages build/deploy jobs, health checks, lighthouserc tests
areas.seams: Any existing high/critical CVE in brikette dependencies will block deploys until remediated

areas.label: **CI: shared app pipeline**
areas.current: No pnpm audit, no TruffleHog in `reusable-app.yml`; caryina and future apps lack both
areas.trigger: Push to branches that trigger caryina (or future app) deploys; also `workflow_dispatch` on caryina which bypasses `ci.yml`
areas.future: `reusable-app.yml` runs `pnpm audit --audit-level=high` AND TruffleHog (`--only-verified`) as pre-deploy jobs; all callers benefit including manual workflow_dispatch runs
areas.seams: `--filter` scoping: the audit should cover the specific app's dependencies; TruffleHog checks full git history

areas.label: **Loop-spec stage definition**
areas.current: S9B has no gate annotation; GATE-LAUNCH-SEC undefined
areas.future: S9B stage annotates `GATE-LAUNCH-SEC` (Hard) as the security QA pass condition; ordering constraints unchanged
areas.seams: Stage-operator-map.json derives from `stage-operator-dictionary.yaml` (not `loop-spec.yaml`); map regeneration is a parity check only — no new stage added here. If dictionary is also bumped, regeneration is required; otherwise note the parity gap.
